title: Configure a Pod to Use a ConfigMap — Configure all key-value pairs in a ConfigMap as container environment variables
text:
Create a ConfigMap containing multiple key-value pairs.
configmap/configmap-multikeys.yaml
apiVersion
:
v1
kind
:
ConfigMap
metadata
:
name
:
special-config
namespace
:
default
data
:
SPECIAL_LEVEL
:
very
SPECIAL_TYPE
:
charm
Create the ConfigMap:
kubectl create -f https://kubernetes.io/examples/configmap/configmap-multikeys.yaml
Use
envFrom
to define all of the ConfigMap's data as container environment variables. The
key from the ConfigMap becomes the environment variable name in the Pod.
pods/pod-configmap-envFrom.yaml
apiVersion
:
v1
kind
:
Pod
metadata
:
name
:
dapi-test-pod
spec
:
containers
:
-
name
:
test-container
image
:
registry.k8s.io/busybox:1.27.2
command
:
[
"/bin/sh"
,
"-c"
,
"env"
]
envFrom
:
-
configMapRef
:
name
:
special-config
restartPolicy
:
Never
Create the Pod:
kubectl create -f https://kubernetes.io/examples/pods/pod-configmap-envFrom.yaml
Now, the Pod's output includes environment variables
SPECIAL_LEVEL=very
and
SPECIAL_TYPE=charm
.
Once you're happy to move on, delete that Pod:
kubectl delete pod dapi-test-pod --now
